children.label: i
children.text: The county of residence of the family.

children.label: I
children.text: Federal disability insurance benefits;

children.label: II
children.text: benefits based on Federal disability status;

children.label: III
children.text: aid under a State plan approved under subchapter XIV (as in effect without regard to the amendment made by section 301 of the Social Security Amendments of 1972);

children.label: IV
children.text: aid or assistance under a State plan approved under subchapter XVI (as in effect without regard to such amendment) by reason of being permanently and totally disabled; or

children.label: V
children.text: supplemental security income benefits under subchapter XVI (as in effect pursuant to such amendment) by reason of disability.

children.label: iii
children.text: The ages of the members of such families.

children.label: iv
children.text: The number of individuals in the family, and the relation of each family member to the head of the family.

children.label: v
children.text: The employment status and earnings of the employed adult in the family.

children.label: vi
children.text: The marital status of the adults in the family, including whether such adults have never married, are widowed, or are divorced.

children.label: vii
children.text: The race and educational level of each adult in the family.

children.label: viii
children.text: The race and educational level of each child in the family.

children.label: ix
children.text: Whether the family received subsidized housing, medical assistance under the State plan approved under subchapter XIX, supplemental nutrition assistance program benefits, or subsidized child care, and if the latter 2, the amount received.

children.label: x
children.text: The number of months that the family has received each type of assistance under the program.

children.label: I
children.text: Education.

children.label: II
children.text: Subsidized private sector employment.

children.label: III
children.text: Unsubsidized employment.

children.label: IV
children.text: Public sector employment, work experience, or community service.

children.label: V
children.text: Job search.

children.label: VI
children.text: Job skills training or on-the-job training.

children.label: VII
children.text: Vocational education.

children.label: xii
children.text: Information necessary to calculate participation rates under .section 607 of this title

children.label: xiii
children.text: The type and amount of assistance received under the program, including the amount of and reason for any reduction of assistance (including sanctions).

children.label: xiv
children.text: Any amount of unearned income received by any member of the family.

children.label: xv
children.text: The citizenship of the members of the family.

children.label: I
children.text: employment;

children.label: II
children.text: marriage;

children.label: III
children.text: the prohibition set forth in ;section 608(a)(7) of this title

children.label: IV
children.text: sanction; or

children.label: V
children.text: State policy.

children.label: xvii
children.text: With respect to each individual in the family who has not attained 20 years of age, whether the individual is a parent of a child in the family.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: i Authority
children.text: A State may comply with subparagraph (A) by submitting disaggregated case record information on a sample of families selected through the use of scientifically acceptable sampling methods approved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Sampling and other methods
children.label: ii Sampling and other methods
children.text: The Secretary shall provide the States with such case sampling plans and data collection procedures as the Secretary deems necessary to produce statistically valid estimates of the performance of State programs funded under this part and any other State programs funded with qualified State expenditures (as defined in ). The Secretary may develop and implement procedures for verifying the quality of data submitted by the States.section 609(a)(7)(B)(i) of this title

children.heading: Report on use of Federal funds to cover administrative costs and overhead
children.label: 2 Report on use of Federal funds to cover administrative costs and overhead
children.text: The report required by paragraph (1) for a fiscal quarter shall include a statement of the percentage of the funds paid to the State under this part for the quarter that are used to cover administrative costs or overhead, with a separate statement of the percentage of such funds that are used to cover administrative costs or overhead incurred for programs operated with funds provided under .section 603(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: Report on State expenditures on programs for needy families
children.label: 3 Report on State expenditures on programs for needy families
children.text: The report required by paragraph (1) for a fiscal quarter shall include a statement of the total amount expended by the State during the quarter on programs for needy families, with a separate statement of the total amount expended by the State during the quarter on programs operated with funds provided under .section 603(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: Report on noncustodial parents participating in work activities
children.label: 4 Report on noncustodial parents participating in work activities
children.text: The report required by paragraph (1) for a fiscal quarter shall include the number of noncustodial parents in the State who participated in work activities (as defined in ) during the quarter, with a separate statement of the number of such parents who participated in programs operated with funds provided under .section 607(d) of this titlesection 603(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: Report on transitional services
children.label: 5 Report on transitional services
children.text: The report required by paragraph (1) for a fiscal quarter shall include the total amount expended by the State during the quarter to provide transitional services to a family that has ceased to receive assistance under this part because of employment, along with a description of such services.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of families and individuals receiving assistance under the State program funded under this part (including the number of 2-parent and 1-parent families);

children.label: B
children.text: the total dollar value of such assistance received by all families; and

children.label: i
children.text: the total number of such families and individuals; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of such families and individuals whose participation in such a program was terminated during a month.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 7 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to define the data elements with respect to which reports are required by this subsection, and shall consult with the Secretary of Labor in defining the data elements with respect to programs operated with funds provided under .section 603(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: Annual reports to Congress by Secretary
children.label: b Annual reports to Congress by Secretary
A
children.text: the participation rates described in ; andsection 607(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: increasing employment and earnings of needy families, and child support collections; and

children.label: ii
children.text: decreasing out-of-wedlock pregnancies and child poverty;
the demographic and financial characteristics of families applying for assistance, families receiving assistance, and families that become ineligible to receive assistance;
the characteristics of each State program funded under this part; and
the trends in employment and earnings of needy families with minor children living at home.

children.heading: Pre-reauthorization State-by-State reports on engagement in additional work activities and expenditures for other benefits and services
children.label: c Pre-reauthorization State-by-State reports on engagement in additional work activities and expenditures for other benefits and services

children.heading: March 2011 report
children.label: i March 2011 report
children.text: Not later than , a report for the period that begins on , and ends on , that contains the information specified in subparagraphs (B) and (C).May 31, 2011March 1, 2011March 31, 2011

children.label: I
children.text: the average monthly numbers for the information specified in subparagraph (B); and

children.label: II
children.text: the information specified in subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Engagement in additional work activities
children.label: B Engagement in additional work activities
I
children.text: that do not qualify as a work activity under but that are otherwise reasonably calculated to help the family move toward self-sufficiency; orsection 607(d) of this title

children.label: aa
children.text: the work-eligible individual did not engage in sufficient hours of the activity;

children.label: bb
children.text: the work-eligible individual has reached the maximum time limit allowed for having participation in the activity counted toward the State’s work participation rate; or

children.label: cc
children.text: the number of work-eligible individuals engaged in such activity exceeds a limitation under such section.

children.label: ii
children.text: Any other information that the Secretary determines appropriate with respect to the information required under clause (i), including if the individual has no hours of participation, the principal reason or reasons for such non-participation.

children.heading: Expenditures on other benefits and services
children.label: C Expenditures on other benefits and services
I
children.text: Federal funds provided under that are (or will be) reported by the State on Form ACF–196 (or any successor form) under the category of other expenditures or the category of benefits or services provided in accordance with the authority provided under ; orsection 603 of this titlesection 604(a)(2) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: State funds expended to meet the requirements of and reported by the State in the category of other expenditures on Form ACF–196 (or any successor form).section 609(a)(7) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: Any other information that the Secretary determines appropriate with respect to the information required under clause (i).

children.heading: Publication of summary and analysis of engagement in additional activities
children.label: 2 Publication of summary and analysis of engagement in additional activities
A
children.text: a summary of the information submitted in the report:

children.label: B
children.text: an analysis statement regarding the extent to which the information changes measures of total engagement in work activities from what was (or will be) reported by the State in the quarterly report submitted under subsection (a) for the comparable period; and

children.label: C
children.text: a narrative describing the most common activities contained in the report that are not countable toward the State participation rates under .section 607 of this title

children.heading: Application of authority to use sampling
children.label: 3 Application of authority to use sampling
children.text: Subparagraph (B) of subsection (a)(1) shall apply to the reports required under paragraph (1) of this subsection in the same manner as subparagraph (B) of subsection (a)(1) applies to reports required under subparagraph (A) of subsection (a)(1).

children.heading: March 2011 report
children.label: A March 2011 report
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the information submitted by eligible States for the March 2011 reporting period under paragraph (1)(A)(i). The report shall include a State-by-State summary and analysis of such information, identification of any States with missing or incomplete reports, and recommendations for such administrative or legislative changes as the Secretary determines are necessary to require eligible States to report the information on a recurring basis.June 30, 2011

children.heading: April-June, 2011 report
children.label: B April-June, 2011 report
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report on the information submitted by eligible States for the April-June 2011 reporting period under paragraph (1)(A)(ii). The report shall include a State-by-State summary and analysis of such information, identification of any States with missing or incomplete reports, and recommendations for such administrative or legislative changes as the Secretary determines are necessary to require eligible States to report the information on a recurring basisSeptember 30, 20111So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

children.heading: Authority for expeditious implementation
children.label: 5 Authority for expeditious implementation
children.text: The requirements of chapter 5 of title 5 (commonly referred to as the “Administrative Procedure Act”) or any other law relating to rulemaking or publication in the Federal Register shall not apply to the issuance of guidance or instructions by the Secretary with respect to the implementation of this subsection to the extent the Secretary determines that compliance with any such requirement would impede the expeditious implementation of this subsection.

children.heading: Data exchange standardization for improved interoperability
children.label: d Data exchange standardization for improved interoperability

children.heading: Designation
children.label: A Designation
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with an interagency work group which shall be established by the Office of Management and Budget, and considering State and tribal perspectives, shall, by rule, designate a data exchange standard for any category of information required to be reported under this part.

children.heading: Data exchange standards must be nonproprietary and interoperable
children.label: B Data exchange standards must be nonproprietary and interoperable
children.text: The data exchange standard designated under subparagraph (A) shall, to the extent practicable, be nonproprietary and interoperable.

children.label: i
children.text: interoperable standards developed and maintained by an international voluntary consensus standards body, as defined by the Office of Management and Budget, such as the International Organization for Standardization;

children.label: ii
children.text: interoperable standards developed and maintained by intergovernmental partnerships, such as the National Information Exchange Model; and

children.label: iii
children.text: interoperable standards developed and maintained by Federal entities with authority over contracting and financial assistance, such as the Federal Acquisition Regulatory Council.

children.heading: Designation
children.label: A Designation
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with an interagency work group established by the Office of Management and Budget, and considering State and tribal perspectives, shall, by rule, designate data exchange standards to govern the data reporting required under this part.

children.label: i
children.text: incorporate a widely-accepted, nonproprietary, searchable, computer-readable format;

children.label: ii
children.text: be consistent with and implement applicable accounting principles; and

children.label: iii
children.text: be capable of being continually upgraded as necessary.

children.heading: Incorporation of nonproprietary standards
children.label: C Incorporation of nonproprietary standards
children.text: In designating reporting standards under this paragraph, the Secretary shall, to the extent practicable, incorporate existing nonproprietary standards, such as the eXtensible Markup Language.

children.heading: Pilot projects for promoting accountability by measuring work outcomes
children.label: e Pilot projects for promoting accountability by measuring work outcomes
A
children.text: the percentage of work-eligible individuals under the State program funded under this part who are in unsubsidized employment during the 2nd quarter after exiting the program;

children.label: B
children.text: the level of earnings of such individuals in the 2nd and 4th quarters after exit; and

children.label: C
children.text: other indicators of family stability and well-being as established by the Secretary.

children.heading: Level of performance benchmark
children.label: 2 Level of performance benchmark
children.text: The Secretary and a State selected under paragraph (1) shall agree to the requisite level of performance on these benchmarks after developing baseline data in the State and comparative data in other States.

children.heading: Failure of State to meet benchmark
children.label: 3 Failure of State to meet benchmark
children.text: If a State fails to meet a measured benchmark standard agreed to under paragraph (2) for 2 successive fiscal years, the State, in order to continue in the pilot shall enter into a plan with the Secretary to achieve the required level of performance or, if mutually agreed to, adjust the benchmark based on new information about the feasibility of meeting such benchmark.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 4 Duration
children.text: The pilot under this subsection shall be in effect for 6 fiscal years, with one year to establish benchmark data and negotiate targets and five years to measure performance against the targets, and shall supersede the requirements under for such fiscal years, notwithstanding any other provision of law.section 607 of this title

children.heading: Application of penalty for failure to reduce assistance for recipients refusing without good cause to work
children.label: 5 Application of penalty for failure to reduce assistance for recipients refusing without good cause to work
children.text: For purposes of , a State operating a pilot must have a system for reducing the amount of assistance payable to a family if an individual refuses, without good cause (including for reasons described in 607(e)(2) of this title), to engage in any such activities as the State has required of such an individual. A State without such a system shall be considered to have failed to comply with the requirements of for so long as the failure to comply continues.section 609(a)(14) of this title2section 607(e) of this titleSo in original. Probably should be preceded by “section”.2

children.heading: Collection of performance data
children.label: 6 Collection of performance data
children.text: Each State selected under paragraph (1), in consultation with the Secretary, shall collect and submit to the Secretary data on the performance of the State operating such a pilot program.

children.heading: Initial report
children.label: A Initial report
children.text: Not later than 12 months after , the Secretary shall submit a report to Congress on the status of the program under this section.June 3, 2023

children.heading: Final report
children.label: B Final report
children.text: Not later than 12 months after the date on which the programs under this section have terminated, the Secretary shall submit a comprehensive report to Congress on outcomes achieved under such programs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each State, in consultation with the Secretary, shall collect and submit to the Secretary the information necessary for each indicator described in paragraph (2), for fiscal year 2025 and each fiscal year thereafter.

children.label: A
children.text: The percentage of individuals who were work-eligible individuals as of the time of exit from the program, who are in unsubsidized employment during the second quarter after the exit.

children.label: B
children.text: The percentage of individuals who were work-eligible individuals who were in unsubsidized employment in the second quarter after the exit, who are also in unsubsidized employment during the fourth quarter after the exit.

children.label: C
children.text: The median earnings of individuals who were work-eligible individuals as of the time of exit from the program, who are in unsubsidized employment during the second quarter after the exit.

children.label: D
children.text: The percentage of individuals who have not attained 24 years of age, are attending high school or enrolled in an equivalency program, and are work-eligible individuals or were work-eligible individuals as of the time of exit from the program, who obtain a high school degree or its recognized equivalent while receiving assistance under the State program funded under this part or within 1 year after the exit.

children.heading: Definition of exit
children.label: 3 Definition of exit
children.text: In paragraph (2), the term “exit” means, with respect to a State program funded under this part, ceases to receive assistance under the program funded by this part.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 4 Regulations
children.text: In order to ensure nationwide comparability of data, the Secretary, after consultation with the Secretary of Labor and with States, shall issue regulations governing the reporting of performance indicators under this subsection.
